title: Runner Envelope
description: Wrapper envelope emitted by every runner skill in this repo (claude, codex, gemini, qwen, cline, and the cline-backed kimi/glm and qwen-backed gemma/minimax shims). All --json responses carry the required keys below on every exit path; individual runners add the documented extension keys and may add further diagnostic keys (additionalProperties is intentionally open).

runner.description: Requested runner identity (claude, codex, gemini, qwen, cline, kimi, glm, gemma, minimax, ...).

effective_runner.description: Runner that actually produced output; equals runner unless a fallback occurred.

effective_model.description: Model that actually answered; equals model unless overridden by the CLI/provider.

effective_provider.description: Provider backing the effective runner (anthropic, openai, google, qwen, z-ai, moonshot, minimax, ...).

auth_ok.description: Auth preflight result: true on a successful run; null when auth was never exercised (missing CLI, invalid input, or a failure before auth could be established); false only when an authentication failure was actually detected. A missing CLI is untested auth, not failed auth — it maps to null, never false.

fallback_reason.description: Why a fallback was used, or null when no fallback occurred. No-fallback runners (qwen, cline, kimi, glm, gemma, minimax) always keep this null.

success.description: Whether the run completed with return_code 0 and no native error.

return_code.description: 0 success; -1 timeout; -2 CLI not found (seat_unavailable); -3 invalid input / native agent or API error / unexpected error. Runner-specific additions: claude-runner emits -4 for bare mode without ANTHROPIC_API_KEY/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN; gemini-runner passes positive agy exit codes through unchanged.

stdout.description: Raw CLI stdout (native stream-json, json, or text). For debugging; prefer agent_message.

stderr.description: CLI stderr plus any native error text appended by the wrapper.

command.description: Shell-quoted command line that was executed.

working_dir.description: Working directory the CLI ran in.

role.description: Role overlay applied (planner, codereviewer, implementer, synthesizer, adversarial, challenger, researcher), or null.

restrict_tools.description: Whether a read-only analysis overlay/sandbox was applied.

chat_recording.description: qwen-backed runners: always false; the wrapper forces --chat-recording=false.

agent_message.description: Clean final answer: trimmed result text for plain runs, or JSON-serialized output for --output-schema (structured_output) runs. Orchestrators should read this instead of parsing stdout.

session_id.description: Session id reported by the native CLI (or recovered from history for cline-backed runners), when available.

native_result.description: Raw result payload from the native result event. May be a string, object, or array; presence indicates the CLI emitted a result event. For --output-schema runs this is the structured object (agent_message is its JSON serialization).

status.description: Failure classification when relevant: seat_unavailable (return_code -2), timeout (return_code -1), or auth_failed (detected authentication failure; forces success=false).

fallback_from.description: The originally requested seat when a fallback runner produced this output (e.g. fallback_from: gemini). Present only on fallback runs; the core of seat-fidelity labeling.

fallback_attempts.description: gemini-runner: the sibling runners tried and skipped before the returned result (including not_installed siblings), so the attempt log is complete even when every fallback was unavailable.

output_json_valid.description: gemini-runner with --output-format json: whether the fence-stripped agent_message parsed as JSON.

finish_reason.description: cline-backed runners: Cline's native finishReason (completed, error, ...) from the terminal run_result event.

native_model_id.description: cline-backed runners: the model that actually answered, read back from the native stream.

native_provider.description: cline-backed runners: the provider that actually answered, read back from the native stream.

native_return_code.description: cline-backed runners: the raw process exit code before return-code normalization.

thinking.description: cline-backed runners: the reasoning-effort level forwarded to cline --thinking, when set.

provider_config_mutated.description: cline-backed runners: true when --model was forwarded without an isolating --data-dir, meaning the user's persisted provider default in ~/.cline/data/settings/providers.json was rewritten as a side effect.

prompt_file.description: Present when exactly one --prompt-file was supplied.

prompt_files.description: Present when more than one --prompt-file was supplied.
